question.text: On NSE, the standard trading cycle for monthly index options includes:

answer.text: Three monthly contracts - near month, next month, and far month
Six monthly contracts at any given time
Twelve monthly contracts covering the entire calendar year

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** NSE offers three monthly option contracts at any time: near month (current), next month, and far month (month after next). As the near-month contract expires, a new far-month contract is introduced.
**The Trap:** While weekly options also exist for benchmark indices, the standard monthly cycle is 3 months.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This 3-month rolling cycle is the same for both futures and options.
**Key Takeaway:** Trading cycle = 3 monthly contracts (near, next, far). New far-month added when near-month expires.
